coreJobStatement: Design, manufacture, and commission rail vehicles that transport passengers and freight safely, reliably, and on schedule
productJobStatement: Help Carbody Fabrication to supply precision structural steel cross-section shapes at specified geometry, dimensional tolerance, and material grade for direct welded integration into rail vehicle carbody and bogie assemblies
anchorLevel: L5 (Structural Steel Profiles within L6b Carbody Fabrication); L4 secondary (Bogie Frame Profiles within L6c)

segments.name: New platform design team
segments.characteristics: Developing new vehicle architecture requiring new profile geometries and material qualification
segments.circumstance: Developing new vehicle architecture requiring new profile geometries and material qualification
segments.alternativesDiffer: Custom profile vs standard structural sections

segments.name: Series production program
segments.characteristics: Running active production program with repeat geometry orders; speed and consistency critical
segments.targetabilityReason: Repeat supply consistency; lead time = shipping only
segments.circumstance: Active production program with repeat geometry orders; speed and consistency critical

segments.name: Fleet upgrade / retrofit
segments.characteristics: Replacing structural members in existing vehicles; geometry constrained by original design
segments.circumstance: Replacing structural members in existing vehicles

segments.name: Freight wagon fabricator
segments.characteristics: High-volume, heavy-duty structural profiles; cost and lead time primary drivers
segments.circumstance: High-volume, heavy-duty structural profiles; cost and lead time primary drivers

segments.name: Tier 1 carbody sub-contractor
segments.characteristics: Fabricates carbody shells on contract for OEMs; must satisfy OEM certification upstream
segments.circumstance: Fabricates carbody shells on contract for OEMs; must satisfy OEM certification requirements

alternatives.name: Custom hot-rolled / cold-drawn special geometry profiles
alternatives.tradeoffs: Custom die NRE (EUR 5-30K/geometry); 6-12 wk tooling lead time for new geometries; min batch ~100 kg; near-net-shape reduces downstream machining. ZOLLERN position.

alternatives.name: Standard hot-rolled structural sections (I, U, T, H, L profiles)
alternatives.tradeoffs: No tooling lead time; commodity sourcing; requires extensive machining/fit-up to achieve custom geometry; significant material waste vs near-net; limited profile type freedom.

alternatives.name: Aluminium alloy extrusions
alternatives.tradeoffs: ~60-70% weight saving vs steel; different weld process required (TIG Al alloy); incompatible with ZOLLERN (C6 material constraint); higher unit cost.

alternatives.name: CNC-machined parts from bar stock or plate
alternatives.tradeoffs: Maximum geometry freedom; no tooling; very high machining cycle time; 40-80% material waste; cost-prohibitive at series volumes.

alternatives.name: Do nothing / redesign to standard stock
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero NRE and zero lead time; forces design team to accept geometry compromises; may increase assembly complexity, weight, or weld count.
